cases.description: Directive Configurable Threshold: forces the main output to zero, so coarse compilation passes but behavioral stimulus points fail.
Directive Configurable Threshold: keeps the main datapath plausible while reporting the wrong metric value.
Directive Configurable Threshold: mishandles reset or initialization, passing steady-state samples but failing startup or recovery checks.
Directive Configurable Threshold: uses a shifted or inverted decision condition that can pass one mode but fails boundary cases.
